title: AI Builders Digest
subtitle: Bilingual edition · 双语对照版

intro.text: 今天有两条主轴值得一起看。第一条是 coding agent 的「主战场转移」——TRQ 把 Claude Code 的 Workflows 推成继 skills 和 subagents 之后最大的升级,Thibault 紧接着在 Codex 一边把企业版做出可以托管网站、改进 plugins、支持在文档/幻灯片/表格上做视觉批注,Amjad 和 Steipete 又分别在同一天宣布跟 Microsoft 的合作。一句话,agent 不再只是单人工具,它在快速变成企业级底座。第二条是「成本结构的变化开始被正视」——Levie 第一次把 token 预算放到 OpEx 高度讲 model routing 的必然性,Rauch 直接宣布 no-code 这个品类的底层假设[code is expensive]已经被 agent 推翻,Garry Tan 则在连发 PR 的间隙提醒:agent 不能替你找到用户。

archive.title: 主战场转移日报
archive.desc: Coding agent 从单兵工具迁移到企业底座,成本结构变化和 SaaS 重估同步发生。

footerNote: Source: Follow Builders curated daily digest. Rebuilt from the 2026-05-29 source feeds.

sections.title: Codex Crosses Into the Knowledge Worker / Codex 跨过工程师的边界
sections.desc: OpenAI 自家公布的 Codex 用户结构变化,叠加 Thibault 对 ChatGPT / agents 长期判断,意味着 Codex 正在从程序员工具变成默认助手。

rewrite: Zara Zhang pulls the most striking data point from OpenAI's latest Codex report: knowledge workers now represent about 20% of Codex users and are adopting it more than 3 times as fast as developers.

The fastest growing task types are Data Analysis (+110% week over week), Research (+37%), and Knowledge work overall. A year ago this product was [for programmers]; today the average user is a non-engineer analyst.

The implication: the unit economics and product surface of Codex are being pulled by a much larger audience than its original user base, which changes pricing, distribution, and feature priorities for OpenAI.
original: From OpenAI's latest Codex report: Knowledge workers now represent about 20% of Codex users and are adopting it more than 3 times as fast as developers.
The fastest growing task types for knowledge workers are: Data Analysis (110% growth week over week); Research (+37%), and Knowledge work overall.

rewrite: Zara Zhang 拎出 OpenAI 内部 Codex 报告里最值得看的一行数字:知识工作者已经占 Codex 用户的 20%,增速是开发者的 3 倍以上。
知识工作者里增速最快的是数据分析(周环比 +110%)、研究(+37%)和广义知识工作。一年前这个产品的对象还是[程序员],现在的典型用户其实是不写代码的分析师。
含义:Codex 的单位经济和产品面正在被一个比最初用户群大得多的群体拉动,OpenAI 的定价、分发和功能优先级都会因此改变。
original: OpenAI 报告:知识工作者占 Codex 用户 20%,增速是开发者的 3 倍。
数据分析周增 110%,研究增 37%,广义知识工作全面爆发。

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux, who leads Codex and ChatGPT at OpenAI, frames the long view in one short post: ChatGPT is already a synonym for AI, and is about to become a synonym for agents too. There is much to build.

The 1100-like / 35-RT endorsement is the team leader publicly buying into the [agent] frame. When the head of the product calls his own roadmap [agents], you can bet the next 12 months of shipping reflect that.
Read alongside Zara's data: Codex is the distribution vehicle for that claim, and the data already shows it.
original: ChatGPT — Whether you understand the name or not. It's here to stay. It's the past, the present, the future. Synonym with AI, soon synonym with agents. Much to build.

rewrite: Thibault Sottiaux(Codex 与 ChatGPT 负责人)用一条短帖讲了一个长期判断:ChatGPT 已经是 AI 的同义词,接下来它还要变成 agent 的同义词。事情还有很多要做的。
1100 赞、35 转推的量级,是产品负责人公开为[agent]这个框架背书。当产品线 leader 自己把路线图叫作[agents],未来 12 个月的发布节奏基本可以按这个口径去预期。
和 Zara 引用的数据放一起看:Codex 就是这个判断的载体,数据已经在支撑它。
original: Thibault:ChatGPT 已是 AI 的同义词,接下来是 agent 的同义词。

rewrite: Thibault announces the daily-work upgrades to Codex: business plan users can now host and share websites directly from Codex, plugins and skills got a major upgrade for broader roles, and you can give feedback to the agent through visual annotations on docs, slides, and sheets.

The visual-annotation detail is the load-bearing one: agents that can read and react to markup on a document are usable by people who do not write code or prompts. That is exactly the non-engineer audience Zara just described.
This is what [Codex becomes a knowledge worker tool] looks like in product form.
original: Tons of goodies for use of codex for day to day work. If you are on a business plan you can now host and share websites, we launched vastly improved plugins and skills for broad roles and you can give feedback to your agent through visual annotations in docs, slides, sheets and more.

rewrite: Thibault 公布了 Codex 在日常工作场景的一批升级:商业版用户可以直接在 Codex 里托管和分享网站,plugins 和 skills 做了一轮大改以适配更广的角色,而且你可以在文档、幻灯片、表格上用[视觉批注]给 agent 直接反馈。
真正起作用的是视觉批注这个细节:能在文档上做高亮批注、让 agent 读懂并修改,这件事只有不会写代码、也不会写 prompt 的人才用得上——正好是 Zara 说的那个非工程师用户群。
这就是[Codex 变成知识工作者工具]这件事在产品层面的具体形态。
original: Codex 商业版支持托管和分享网站,plugins 与 skills 大改,支持文档/幻灯片/表格视觉批注反馈。

sections.title: Claude Code Workflows Go Mainstream / Claude Code 工作流走向主流
sections.desc: TRQ 把 Workflows 推成自 skills / subagents 之后最大的升级,加上他在 Claude Blog 的同步发文,Claude Code 正在从[写代码的 REPL]变成[跑长任务的调度器]。

rewrite: Thariq's headline take: Workflows are the biggest upgrade to Claude Code's capabilities since skills and subagents. He went deep with Sid to figure out best practices, examples, and more.

What excites him most is the non-technical tasks it unlocks — the same surface TRQ has been championing: drop a folder of files, let the agent script + render + iterate. A workflow lets that pattern run for hours, unattended, with explicit human checkpoints.
3339 likes / 236 RTs — this is the developer community signaling that [agent as long-running coworker] is now the model, not the demo.
original: Workflows are the biggest upgrade to Claude Code's capabilities since skills and subagents. I dove deep into it with @sidbid to figure out best practices, examples and more. I'm particularly excited about the non-technical tasks it enables for Claude Code.

rewrite: Thariq 的核心判断:Workflows 是 Claude Code 自 skills 和 subagents 以来最大的一次升级。他跟 Sid 一起深入做了一轮最佳实践、案例和使用模式。
他最兴奋的是它解锁了非技术任务——也就是 TRQ 一直在推的那个模式:把文件夹丢进去,让 agent 写脚本、渲染 HTML、反复迭代。Workflows 让这个模式可以无人值守跑上几个小时,带明确的人工检查点。
3339 赞、236 转推——开发者社区在用投票说[agent 是能跑长任务的同事],不再是演示。
original: TRQ:Workflows 是 Claude Code 自 skills/subagents 后最大升级,最让人兴奋的是非技术任务场景。

rewrite: Companion to the tweet above: TRQ points readers to the official Claude Blog for the full Workflows write-up. The blog post is the canonical place to learn the new mental model.
The dual-channel pattern (Twitter hype + blog depth) is the same playbook that made skills and subagents stick. Workflows is being installed as a default workflow category, not a side feature.
original: This is also available on the Claude Blog! https://t.co/ScXhdyz6YI

rewrite: 跟上一条配对:TRQ 指向 Claude 官方博客的 Workflows 完整文章,那里才是新思维模型的权威来源。
Twitter 制造氛围 + 博客讲深度的双渠道打法,跟当初 skills 和 subagents 走红的套路一样。Workflows 正在被塑造成一种默认的工作流分类,不是一个边角功能。
original: TRQ 指向 Claude Blog 的 Workflows 完整说明。

sections.title: Model Routing & the Cost Story / 模型路由与成本叙事
sections.desc: Token 预算开始进 OpEx,no-code 的底层假设被推翻——成本结构和工具分层正在被一起重写。

rewrite: Aaron Levie names the inevitability: as token budgets take a larger share of operating expenses, model routing becomes the inevitable conclusion. This is also one of the biggest areas of differentiation for the applied AI layer.

The reason: a domain knows its own work patterns, and a smart router can pick the cheap model for 80% of calls and the frontier model for the 20% that actually need it. That is the applied layer's defensible moat — not access to the model.
Read this against the open-model frontier: a router is more valuable the more models you can route between.
original: As token budgets take on a larger part of operating expenses over time, model routing is the inevitable conclusion. This is also one of the biggest areas of differentiation for the applied AI layer over time. By understanding the different work patterns in your domain, and having a router pick the right model for the right call, you can drive down cost significantly.

rewrite: Aaron Levie 把一件正在发生的事点出来了:token 预算在 OpEx 里占比越大,model routing 就越会成为必然结论。这也是应用 AI 层最大的差异化战场之一。
原因是:一个领域最了解自己的工作模式,一个聪明的路由器可以让 80% 的调用走便宜模型,20% 真正需要前沿模型的请求才走贵的。这就是应用层的防御性护城河——不是模型的访问权,而是路由的智能。
跟开源模型的前沿放在一起读:能路由的模型越多,路由器本身越值钱。
original: Levie:token 预算进 OpEx 后,model routing 是必然结论,也是应用层最大差异化。

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch declares the death of an entire software category: [no-code]. The original category was built on the premise that code is expensive, difficult, and scarce. Coding agents have forever changed the equation — code is now cheap, easy, and abundant.

The thread continues into education (start with the language, the linguistic surface is your roadmap, human language is the new API) and into the rise of agent-first IDEs (Conductor as an ADE, agents taking remote dev mainstream).
Read with Levie: the cost story is not just about cheaper model calls, it is about an entire stack of assumptions being unwound.
original: YES-CODE. An entire category of software, 'no-code', was built under the presumption that code is expensive, difficult, and scarce. Coding agents have forever changed the equation. Code is now cheap, easy, and abundant.

rewrite: Guillermo Rauch 宣布了一整个软件品类的死亡:no-code。这个品类原本的假设是[code 昂贵、稀缺、难写]。coding agent 永远地改写了这个等式——code 现在便宜、好写、充裕。
他的推文线继续展开到教育(从语言入手,语言表面就是路线图,人类语言是新的 API),以及 agent-first IDE 的崛起(Conductor 是为 agent 而生的 ADE,agent 把远程开发推上主流)。
跟 Levie 一起读:成本故事不只是模型调用变便宜,而是整整一堆假设在被拆掉。
original: Rauch:no-code 假设 code 稀缺,agent 让 code 充裕,品类基础没了。

sections.title: Agents Go Enterprise / Agent 进入企业级
sections.desc: 同一天两个独立的 enterprise 大单:Steipete 把 OpenClaw 带进 Microsoft,Amjad 把 Replit 跟 Microsoft Fabric 接通。

rewrite: Steinberger announces: it's been a privilege to work with Microsoft to bring claws to enterprises. 3400 likes / 234 RTs is a strong signal that the OpenClaw / [agent on your desktop] story is now landing at the enterprise level.
The partnership gives OpenClaw the kind of distribution that a startup cannot buy: trusted vendor status, IT-managed deployment, the Microsoft security review.
Combined with the other Steinberger post (observability and verifiable workspaces going into OpenClaw), this looks like a full enterprise posture being assembled piece by piece.
original: Such a privilege to work with Microsoft to bring claws to enterprises!

rewrite: Steinberger 宣布:跟 Microsoft 合作把 claws 带进企业。3400 赞、234 转推的量级,说明 OpenClaw 和[agent 跑在桌面上]这个故事正在企业级层面落地。
这次合作给了 OpenClaw 一种创业公司买不到的分发:可信供应商身份、IT 部门可以管的部署、Microsoft 的安全审查。
配合他另一条推文(把可观测性和可验证 workspace 装进 OpenClaw),看起来是一整套企业级姿态在一点点拼起来。
original: Steipete:跟 Microsoft 合作把 OpenClaw 带进企业。

rewrite: Amjad Masad announces a partnership with Microsoft: the Replit team is enabling everyone in the enterprise to build and deploy safe and secure Fabric data apps, on top of Microsoft's new Rayfin SDK.

Fabric is Microsoft's enterprise data platform; pairing it with Replit's agentic app builder means a business analyst can describe a data app and have it built and deployed into the existing data stack, without leaving the Microsoft estate.

Read with Steipete: in the same 24 hours, two independent agentic platforms both landed Microsoft enterprise partnerships. That is not coincidence — Microsoft is making its bet on [agent as the new app layer].
original: Excited to partner with @Microsoft to enable everyone in the enterprise to build and deploy safe & secure Fabric data apps. This is possible thanks to Microsoft's new Rayfin SDK.

rewrite: Amjad Masad 宣布跟 Microsoft 的合作:Replit 团队要让企业里的每个人都能在 Microsoft 新出的 Rayfin SDK 上,构建和部署安全合规的 Fabric 数据应用。
Fabric 是 Microsoft 的企业数据平台;把它跟 Replit 的 agentic 应用构建器接上,意味着一个业务分析师描述一个数据应用,就能直接构建并部署到现有的数据栈里,不离开 Microsoft 的体系。
跟 Steipete 那条放一起读:24 小时内,两个独立的 agentic 平台都落地了 Microsoft 企业合作。这不是巧合——Microsoft 在押[agent 是新的应用层]。
original: Amjad:Replit 联手 Microsoft,基于 Rayfin SDK 在 Fabric 上构建部署数据应用。

sections.title: The Model Frontier Keeps Moving / 模型前沿继续往前推
sections.desc: Gemini 把 Thinking Levels 全面铺开,SWE 基准被质疑,VWE/ViBench 等更接近真实应用的评测开始被点名。

rewrite: Josh Woodward (VP at Google, leading Gemini App, AI Studio, Labs) closes a long-standing papercut: Thinking Levels are now available on Gemini across Web, iOS, and Android.

215 likes on a feature-shipment post is mild, but the move is significant: parity in reasoning controls across surfaces means Google is treating [how hard should the model think] as a first-class user-facing axis, not a developer-only setting.
Read against Levie: a router that can pick thinking levels is meaningfully more powerful than one that just picks models.
original: ✅ Papercut fixed: Thinking Levels are now available on Gemini across Web, iOS, and Android.

rewrite: Josh Woodward(Google VP,负责 Gemini App / AI Studio / Labs)关掉了一个长期 papercut:Thinking Levels 在 Web、iOS、Android 全端可用。
一条功能发布推能拿到 215 赞不算夸张,但这一步的份量在:跨端的推理档位对齐,意味着 Google 把[模型该想多深]当成一个面向用户的一等控制轴,不是只给开发者用的开关。
跟 Levie 一起读:一个能选 thinking level 的路由器,比一个只选模型的路由器强一个量级。
original: Gemini Thinking Levels 已在 Web/iOS/Android 全面铺开。

rewrite: Amjad argues that SWE benchmarks don't necessarily capture app-building capabilities, and points to ViBench as a better measure.

The claim lines up with what builders have been saying for months: the leaderboard models on SWE-bench are not always the models that ship the best end-to-end apps. The community is starting to look past the headline numbers.
Read with the model-routing story: a routing decision based only on SWE-bench scores will systematically pick the wrong model for app-building work.
original: SWE benchmarks don't necessarily capture app building capabilities. ViBench does.

rewrite: Amjad 提出一个观点:SWE 基准并不一定能反映 app 构建能力,VWE/ViBench 才更接近真实。
这个判断跟 builder 圈里这几个月的体感对得上:在 SWE-bench 上领先的模型,未必是端到端 app 构建效果最好的那个。社区开始不只看头条数字了。
跟 model routing 那条放一起:只按 SWE-bench 排名的路由决策,系统性地选错 app 构建工作的模型。
original: Amjad:SWE 基准不反映 app 构建能力,ViBench 更接近真实。

sections.title: Builder Pace & SaaS Reality Check / Builder 节奏与 SaaS 现实
sections.desc: Garry Tan 一天连发,Nikunj 提醒别把单一因素当护城河,Peter Yang 谈 Devin 和 SaaS 死活,Builder 的当下心态一览。

rewrite: Garry Tan: GBrain is the agentic swiss army knife for retrieval and memory. 37 likes is low for a Garry post, but this is a tooling signal — YC's president is naming an open-source agent memory / retrieval project as a daily tool.

The interesting move: Tan is publicly installing an open-source memory layer into his own workflow. For a Y Combinator president to do that is a very different kind of endorsement than a VC tweeting about a portfolio company.
Read with Steipete's autoreview (last week) and Peter Yang's Devin piece below: the builder stack now has open-source memory, automated review, and opinionated agents as discrete pieces.
original: GBrain is the agentic swiss army knife for retrieval and memory.

rewrite: Garry Tan:GBrain 是一个用于检索和记忆的 agentic 瑞士军刀。37 赞对 Garry 来说不算高,但这是个工具信号——YC 的总裁把一个开源 agent 记忆/检索项目当成日常工具来用。
有意思的点在于:Tan 公开把一个开源记忆层装进了自己的工作流。YC 总裁做这件事,跟 VC 发推讲自己投的公司,完全不是一种背书。
跟 Steipete 的 autoreview(上周)和 Peter Yang 谈 Devin 那条放一起读:builder 工具栈已经由开源记忆、自动 review、立场鲜明的 agent 这些独立模块拼起来了。
original: Garry:GBrain 是用于检索和记忆的 agentic 瑞士军刀。

rewrite: Peter Yang gives the Devin / Windsurf team credit for staying disciplined through the ups and downs and keeping at it. He knows many AI-native builders who love Devin now, and wants to find time to try it himself.
The market signal: in 18 months, [agentic dev tool] went from [AGI demo] to [thing serious builders have an opinion on]. The category is now settled enough for measured praise.
The follow-up thought: if Devin is winning builders back, the original [AGI or bust] framing was wrong — the right framing was always [reliable engineering coworker].
original: Have to give the Devin/Windsurf team flowers for staying disciplined through the ups and downs and keeping at it. I know many AI native builders who love Devin now. Will hopefully find some more time to try it myself.

rewrite: Peter Yang 给 Devin / Windsurf 团队点赞,认为他们顶住了起起伏伏,一直在坚持做下来。他认识不少 AI native 的 builder 现在很喜欢 Devin,自己也想找时间再亲自试一下。
市场信号:18 个月里,[agentic 开发工具]从[AGI 演示]走到了[严肃 builder 有真实评价]的阶段。这个品类已经稳到可以给出有节制的赞许。
顺着想:如果 Devin 能把 builder 赢回来,说明一开始[AGI 一举搞定]的框架是错的——对的路一直是[靠谱的工程同事]。
original: Peter Yang 给 Devin 团队点赞:经得起起伏,builder 圈正在回流。

rewrite: Peter Yang's SaaS read: SaaS is not dead, but the simple, narrow SaaS is in trouble. Larger enterprise SaaS that can do multiple jobs (Figma) is probably fine. A simple SaaS for a narrow use case is harder to monetize because AI skills can often solve the same problem.

This is Rauch's YES-CODE frame, but for a different audience. Peter is talking to would-be SaaS founders, not builders: if your only moat is [a single function delivered through a web form], an agent skill will eat it.

The implication for the next 12 months: the B2B SaaS pipeline is going to bifurcate — multi-job platforms with embedded agents, on one side; thin point-solutions, on the other, under serious pressure.
original: People are saying SaaS is not dead. I think larger enterprise SaaS that can do multiple jobs are probably fine (e.g., Figma). But if you're building a simple SaaS for a narrow use case, I think it's harder to monetize now because: 1. AI skills can often solve the same problem.

rewrite: Peter Yang 对 SaaS 的判断:SaaS 没死,但简单、窄场景的 SaaS 日子难过。能做多件事的大型企业级 SaaS(Figma 这种)应该还行。如果只做一个窄场景的简单 SaaS,现在越来越难变现,原因之一是 AI skills 经常能直接解决同类问题。
这是 Rauch 那条 YES-CODE 的换一种说法,但面向的是潜在 SaaS 创始人,不是 builder:如果你的护城河只是[一个功能 + 一个网页表单],agent skill 会吃掉你。
对接下来 12 个月的含义:B2B SaaS 赛道会两极分化——一边是内嵌 agent 的多任务平台,另一边是窄场景的薄点工具,后者会承压。
original: Peter Yang:多任务企业 SaaS 没事,窄场景简单 SaaS 被 AI skills 挤压。

rewrite: Nikunj Kothari: the best founders treat the following as a necessary component of their business but NOT the entire business — AI / timing, funding, distribution / hype, market, product, revenue. He is seeing too many pitches that tout only one of these as the core reason.

The single-claim pitch is the AI-era disease. [We have the best model] or [we are first to market] or [we have viral distribution] is, on its own, a 2021-vintage pitch that the current cycle is rewarding less.
Read with Peter Yang's SaaS piece: in a market where thin moats are being eaten, the pitch that names a single component as the moat is the pitch that is least likely to fund.
original: The best founders treat the following as a necessary component of their business but NOT the entire business: > AI / timing / why now > Funding > Distribution / hype > Market > Product > Revenue. I'm seeing too many pitches that are touting only one of these as the core reason.

rewrite: Nikunj Kothari:最好的创始人把以下每一项都当作必要条件,而不是充分条件——AI / 时机 / 为何是现在、融资、分发 / 话题度、市场、产品、收入。他看到太多 pitch 只把其中一项讲成核心。
单点论调的 pitch 是 AI 时代的流行病。[我们模型最好]、[我们第一个入场]、[我们有病毒式分发]——单拎出来,这都是 2021 年那一代 pitch,这一周期对它们的奖励越来越少。
跟 Peter Yang 那篇 SaaS 现实放一起读:在薄护城河被吃掉的当下,把单一组件当护城河讲的 pitch,正是最难融到钱的 pitch。
original: Nikunj:AI/时机、融资、分发、市场、产品、收入都只是必要条件,不是充分条件。

sections.title: Podcast: Listen Labs and AI-First Customer Research / 播客:Listen Labs 的 AI 一手客户研究
sections.desc: Training Data 播客这一期请到 Listen Labs 创始人 Alfred Wahlforss:一年时间拿到 Fortune 500 中 20% 客户,靠的是 AI 跑大规模语音访谈。

cards.authorTag: AI 工程与训练实践播客

rewrite: Alfred Wahlforss, founder and CEO of Listen Labs, is on Training Data this week. Listen is an AI-first customer research platform that can run thousands of voice interviews simultaneously. A year after launch, Listen serves 20% of the Fortune 500 — Microsoft, Anthropic, Sweetgreen, NBC are customers.

The core strategy: get to a billion people in the audience, then stratify by what each person is an expert on — even something as niche as sneakers. Find the early-adopter / influencer segment for the topic, interview them first, and the insights are far more valuable than a generic sample.

The platform builds persistent profiles of people as it runs more interviews, then lets you search and find the right person to talk to. The competitive moat is the [interview graph]: each new interview makes every future one more accurate.
original: Our goal is to get to a billion people in our audience and then to be able to stratify and know what exactly is this person an expert on. And it might be, you know, even something like sneakers. You have some people who are influencers and kind of early adopters. And if you're able to find that audience and interview them first, the insights are much more valuable.
We build profiles of people as we do more interviews in the platform, and then we can search and find the right person.

rewrite: Listen Labs 的创始人兼 CEO Alfred Wahlforss 上了这一期 Training Data。Listen 是一个 AI 一手的客户研究平台,可以同时跑几千场语音访谈。发布一年,客户已经覆盖 Fortune 500 的 20%——Microsoft、Anthropic、Sweetgreen、NBC 都在用。
核心策略:把用户规模做到十亿,然后按[这个人在哪个细分领域是专家]做分层——哪怕是球鞋这种小领域也行。找到该话题的早期采用者和影响者,先访谈他们,洞察价值远高于一个普通抽样。
平台会在访谈过程中持续给每个人建档,之后可以直接搜索找到最合适的人去谈。竞争护城河就是那张[访谈图谱]:每多跑一场访谈,未来的每一场都更准。
original: Listen 目标:十亿用户,按细分专长分层,先访谈早期采用者,洞察更值钱。
访谈图谱随规模增长,搜索可定位最合适的人。
